prerequisites: A Pinecone account and a pre-created Pinecone index whose dimensionality matches the embedding model you choose (for example 1536 for OpenAI, 1024 for Cohere).
A Pinecone API key for the target project, created in the Pinecone console.
The Pinecone index name and environment/region (for example us-east-1-aws) for the indexing configuration.
An embedding provider account and API key matching your chosen embedder (OpenAI, Cohere, or an OpenAI-compatible service), unless using the built-in Fake embedder for testing.
Values for the required embedding, indexing, and processing configuration objects.

authMethods.name: Pinecone API key (indexing.pinecone_key)
authMethods.summary: Pinecone authenticates every request with a project-scoped API key. Create the key in the Pinecone console under your project's API Keys page; a generated key has ReadWrite access to all indexes in that project. The connector sends it for indexing operations, and Pinecone HTTP requests carry it in the Api-Key header. Supply this value as the indexing.pinecone_key secret.

authMethods.name: OpenAI embedding API key (embedding.openai_key)
authMethods.summary: When the embedding provider is set to OpenAI, the connector calls OpenAI to generate vector embeddings (1536 dimensions) and authenticates with an OpenAI API key. Provide it as the embedding.openai_key secret.

authMethods.name: Cohere embedding API key (embedding.cohere_key)
authMethods.summary: When the embedding provider is set to Cohere, the connector calls Cohere to generate vector embeddings (1024 dimensions) and authenticates with a Cohere API key. Provide it as the embedding.cohere_key secret.

authMethods.name: OpenAI-compatible embedding API key (embedding.api_key)
authMethods.summary: When using an OpenAI-compatible embedding service, the connector authenticates to that service with its API key. Provide it as the embedding.api_key secret.

setupSteps.title: Create a Pinecone API key
setupSteps.body: Sign in to the Pinecone console and open the API Keys page for your project (https://app.pinecone.io/organizations/-/projects/-/keys). Create a new API key and copy it. The key is project-specific and has ReadWrite access to all indexes in that project. Use this value for indexing.pinecone_key.

setupSteps.title: Create the target Pinecone index
setupSteps.body: In Pinecone, create an index ahead of time with a dimensionality that matches the embedding model you will use (for example 1536 for OpenAI or 1024 for Cohere). Note the index name and the environment/region (for example us-east-1-aws). These, together with the API key, populate the required indexing configuration object.

setupSteps.title: Configure the embedding provider
setupSteps.body: In the required embedding object, choose your embedding provider (OpenAI, Cohere, an OpenAI-compatible service, or Fake for testing) and supply the matching API key: embedding.openai_key for OpenAI, embedding.cohere_key for Cohere, or embedding.api_key for an OpenAI-compatible service. Ensure the chosen model's output dimensions match the index you created.

setupSteps.title: Configure processing and optional fields
setupSteps.body: In the required processing object, specify the text field(s) to embed, any metadata fields to attach, and the chunk length (up to 8191 tokens per chunk). Optionally set omit_raw_text to exclude storing the raw source text alongside the vectors.
